prerequisites: A ConvertKit (now Kit) account on a plan eligible for API access.
Decide which authentication path to use: a V4 API key for personal-account automation, V4 OAuth for a registered Kit App Store app, or the deprecated V3 API key/secret pair.
For OAuth: a registered app in the Kit dashboard (App Store) with API access enabled, plus configured Authorization URL and Redirect URIs, which yields the client_id and client_secret.
A start_date value, since the connector requires start_date to bound how far back records are synced.
Credentials to populate the required credentials config object (and the optional api_secret field when using V3).

authMethods.name: V4 API Key
authMethods.summary: Kit V4 supports API key authentication for personal-account automation. Create the key in the Developer tab of your Kit account settings and save it immediately, since it cannot be retrieved again after leaving the screen. The key is passed on each request in an X-Kit-Api-Key header and maps to the credentials.api_key secret field.

authMethods.name: V4 OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Kit V4 supports the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Grant for Kit App Store apps, with a refresh-token flow for web servers and a PKCE flow for SPA/mobile apps. Registering an app provides the client_id and client_secret; the flow returns an access_token (used as a Bearer token) and a single-use refresh_token. These map to the credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, credentials.access_token, and credentials.refresh_token secret fields.

authMethods.name: V3 API Key and Secret (legacy)
authMethods.summary: The legacy Kit/ConvertKit V3 API uses an API Key and API Secret. V3 is still available but is deprecated and scheduled to be sunset. In this connector the V3 key maps to credentials.api_key and the secret maps to the optional api_secret config field; prefer V4 for new setups.

setupSteps.title: Confirm API eligibility and choose a version
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your Kit (ConvertKit) account and confirm the plan is eligible for API use. Choose V4 (current) for new integrations; V3 is deprecated and will be sunset. Then pick API key or OAuth based on whether you are automating your own account or building or connecting an app.

setupSteps.title: Create a V4 API key (API key path)
setupSteps.body: In your Kit account settings, open the Developer tab and create an API key. Copy and store the key right away, because it cannot be viewed again after you leave the screen. This value goes into credentials.api_key and is sent as the X-Kit-Api-Key header.

setupSteps.title: Register an OAuth app and obtain tokens (OAuth path)
setupSteps.body: Register an app in the Kit dashboard (App Store), enable API access, and configure the Authorization URL and Redirect URIs. Use the resulting client_id and client_secret to run the Authorization Code Grant (refresh-token flow for servers, PKCE for SPA/mobile) and exchange the authorization code for an access_token and refresh_token. Supply client_id, client_secret, access_token, and refresh_token in the credentials object.

setupSteps.title: Provide legacy V3 credentials (only if using V3)
setupSteps.body: If you must use the deprecated V3 API, supply the V3 API Key as credentials.api_key and the V3 API Secret in the optional api_secret config field.

setupSteps.title: Fill the connector configuration
setupSteps.body: Populate the required credentials object with the chosen credentials and set the required start_date to bound the sync window. The api_secret field is optional and only needed for V3.

sources.title: Kit (ConvertKit) API Reference Overview (V4 current, V3 deprecated)
